forecast-period.text: A high pressure system in the Tasman Sea maintains a ridge over SA. A cold front will move across the Bight on Friday with a weak high pressure system developing over southern Australia over the weekend. A trough of low pressure moves into the far west on Monday as an associated low pressure system moves into the Bight.
Wind and wave forecasts are averages. Wind gusts can be 40 per cent stronger than the forecast, and stronger still in squalls and thunderstorms. Maximum waves can be twice the forecast height.
For latest warnings go to www.bom.gov.au, subscribe to RSS feeds, call 1300 659 210* or listen for warnings on relevant TV and radio broadcasts.
* Calls to 1300 numbers cost around 27.5c incl. GST, higher from mobiles or public phones. Marine forecasts are also available on VHF and HF radio. View the radio schedule on www.bom.gov.au/marine
Check the latest Coastal Waters Forecasts for information on wind, wave and weather conditions for neighbouring coastal zones.
Northeasterly about 10 knots increasing to 10 to 15 knots in the evening. Winds reaching up to 20 knots south of Cowell to Wallaroo in the late evening.
Below 1 metre.
South to southwesterly below 1 metre, increasing to 1 to 2 metres south of Cowell to Wallaroo.
Cloudy.

forecast-period.warning-summary: Strong Wind Warning for Friday for Spencer Gulf
forecast-period.text: Northeasterly 15 to 20 knots turning northerly 15 to 25 knots before dawn. Winds reaching up to 30 knots during the day.
1 to 1.5 metres, increasing to 1.5 to 2.5 metres during the morning.
South to southwesterly below 1 metre, increasing to 1 to 1.5 metres south of Cowell to Wallaroo.
Partly cloudy.
North to northwesterly 20 to 30 knots decreasing to 15 to 25 knots before dawn then shifting south to southwesterly 15 to 20 knots during the morning.
1.5 to 2.5 metres, decreasing to 1 to 1.5 metres during the morning.
South to southwesterly below 1 metre, increasing to 1 to 2 metres south of Cowell to Wallaroo.
Cloudy. Near 100% chance of rain.
Southeasterly 10 to 15 knots tending easterly during the morning then tending northeasterly during the evening.
Below 1 metre.
South to southwesterly around 1 metre, increasing to 1 to 2 metres south of Cowell to Wallaroo.
Partly cloudy.
Northeasterly about 10 knots.
Below 0.5 metres.
South to southwesterly below 1 metre.
Partly cloudy.

forecast-period.warning-summary: Strong Wind Warning for Friday for Gulf St Vincent
forecast-period.text: Northeasterly 10 to 15 knots tending northerly 15 to 25 knots in the early morning then increasing to 20 to 30 knots in the evening.
Around 1 metre, increasing to 1 to 2 metres during the morning.
South to southwesterly below 1 metre.
Cloudy.
Northerly 20 to 30 knots shifting south to southwesterly 10 to 15 knots in the morning.
1 to 2 metres, decreasing to 1 metre during the morning.
South to southwesterly below 1 metre.
Cloudy. Near 100% chance of rain.
Variable about 10 knots becoming easterly 10 to 15 knots during the afternoon then tending northeasterly during the evening.
Below 1 metre.
South to southwesterly below 1 metre.
Mostly sunny.
North to northeasterly 10 to 15 knots.
Around 1 metre.
Southwesterly 3 to 4 metres, decreasing to 3 metres later in the evening.
Cloudy.

forecast-period.warning-summary: Strong Wind Warning for Friday for Central Coast
forecast-period.text: North to northeasterly 15 to 20 knots, reaching up to 30 knots during the day. Winds turning northwesterly 20 to 25 knots in the evening.
1 to 1.5 metres, increasing to 2 to 3 metres during the morning.
Southwesterly 3 metres, increasing to 3 to 4 metres by early evening.
Cloudy. 95% chance of rain in the west, 60% chance elsewhere.
West to northwesterly 20 to 25 knots turning southwesterly 15 to 20 knots early in the morning.
1.5 to 2.5 metres, decreasing to 1 to 1.5 metres during the morning, then decreasing below 1 metre by early evening.
Southwesterly 3 to 4 metres.
Partly cloudy. 95% chance of rain.
Variable below 10 knots becoming northeasterly 10 to 15 knots during the morning.
Below 1 metre.
Southwesterly 3 to 4 metres, decreasing to 3 metres during the afternoon or evening.
Cloudy.
Northeasterly about 10 knots.
Below 1 metre.
West to southwesterly 1.5 to 2.5 metres.
Cloudy.

forecast-period.warning-summary: Strong Wind Warning for Friday for Investigator Strait
forecast-period.text: Northeasterly 15 to 20 knots turning northerly 20 to 25 knots in the early morning. Winds reaching up to 30 knots during the day.
Below 1 metre, increasing to 1 to 1.5 metres during the morning, then increasing to 1.5 to 2.5 metres around midday.
West to southwesterly 1 to 2 metres, decreasing to 1 to 1.5 metres around midday, then increasing to 1 to 2 metres later in the evening.
Cloudy.
North to northwesterly 20 to 30 knots shifting southwesterly 10 to 15 knots in the early morning.
1.5 to 2.5 metres, decreasing to 1 metre during the morning.
West to southwesterly 1 to 2 metres.
Partly cloudy. Near 100% chance of rain.
Variable below 10 knots becoming east to northeasterly 10 to 15 knots during the morning.
Below 1 metre.
West to southwesterly 1 to 2 metres, decreasing to 1 to 1.5 metres during the afternoon.
Partly cloudy.
